sourceMovie.title: The Land Before Time
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Land Before Time works as a family movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The family and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lion King
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Land Before Time clicked for you, The Lion King (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ice Age
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Land Before Time clicked for you, Ice Age (2002) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Land Before Time?
faq.answer: Try The Lion King, Ice Age, How to Train Your Dragon 2—each shares DNA with The Land Before Time while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Land Before Time?
faq.answer: The Land Before Time (1988) is best described as Family, Animation, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Land Before Time part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Land Before Time Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
